seoTitle: How to Group Tasks in ClickUp for Cleaner Lists
metaDescription: Learn how to group tasks in ClickUp by status, assignee, priority, or custom fields, and save the layout as a shared view.
intro: A long flat task list gets hard to scan once a project grows, but grouping lets you slice that same list by whatever attribute matters most right now. This guide covers opening the Group By controls, switching between structural groupings like priority or assignee, and using custom field values or dual-level grouping to build more specialized views your team can rely on.

steps.heading: Open a List view
steps.body: Launch an active project list and locate the upper toolbar where filtering and grouping controls live.

steps.heading: Click the Group dropdown
steps.body: Click the Group button to open the multi-variable sorting options available for your list.

steps.heading: Select a core parameter
steps.body: Choose an option like Priority or Assignee to watch your tasks instantly rearrange into grouped sections.

steps.heading: Group by a custom field
steps.body: Access custom fields from the sub-menu to group tasks by brand-specific metrics relevant to your workflow.

steps.heading: Enable dual-level grouping
steps.body: Activate second-level grouping to layer additional categories, like team rows over individual resource allocations.

steps.heading: Save the grouped view
steps.body: Click the Save View option to permanently pin your grouped layout to the Space navigation for the whole team to use.

faq.question: Can I group tasks by more than one attribute at once?
faq.answer: Yes, ClickUp supports dual-level grouping so you can layer a second category on top of your primary grouping without duplicating rows.

faq.question: Will grouped views update automatically as tasks change?
faq.answer: Yes, tasks rearrange automatically as their status, assignee, or custom field values change to match the grouping you've selected.

faq.question: Can I move a task between groups by dragging it?
faq.answer: Yes, click-and-drag handles let you move tasks between different grouped sections directly.

faq.question: Does saving a grouped view affect other team members?
faq.answer: Saving a view pins the grouped layout to your Space navigation, making it accessible to your team depending on your sharing settings.
